title: Dispatch tools accept optional schema for structured output
motivation: Roundtable dispatch tools currently emit freeform markdown from each panelist. Extracting placement votes, confidence ratings, or counter-arguments across 7 panelists requires manual re-reading. Allowing callers to pass an optional JSON-Schema-lite shape lets panelists return validated structured output, making jq-style extraction trivial. When the parameter is omitted, behavior is unchanged — current freeform markdown remains the default — so the feature is purely additive.

included: JSON-Schema-lite subset definition and parser supporting typed scalar fields with optional enum constraints.
Schema-to-prompt-suffix construction so panelists know what shape to emit.
Per-panelist response validation against the schema with structured error surfacing on failure.
Wiring the schema parameter into all five dispatch MCP tools (canvass, deliberate, blueprint, critique, crosscheck).
excluded: Full JSON Schema support (only a typed-scalar + enum subset is in scope).
Silent retry on validation failure — failures surface raw text plus structured error to the caller.
CLI flag surface — dispatch tools are MCP-invoked; the parameter is an MCP input field, not a CLI flag.

design_facts.decision: Support typed scalar fields (string/number/bool) with optional enum constraints, modeled after the feedback example {placement: a|b|c|d, deliverable: markdown, confidence: low|med|high, counter_argument: text}.
design_facts.rationale: Minimum viable subset to cover the placement-vote / confidence / counter-argument extraction use case driving the request.

design_facts.decision: When schema is omitted, dispatch tools behave exactly as today (freeform markdown).
design_facts.rationale: Adding the param must be non-breaking for existing callers.

design_facts.decision: On validation failure, surface raw panelist text with a structured error; do not silently retry.
Schema is an MCP tool input parameter, not a CLI flag — dispatch tools are MCP-invoked only.
Panelists wrap their structured response in a fenced JSON code block; the validator extracts the last fenced JSON block in the response.
design_facts.rationale: Aligns with how Claude/Gemini/Codex reliably emit JSON; permits panelists to write narrative before the final payload.

design_facts.decision: Add Result.Structured (*json.RawMessage, omitempty) and Result.StructuredError (*ValidationError, omitempty); both nil on the omitted-schema path so the wire format is byte-equivalent to current output.
design_facts.rationale: Keeps backwards compatibility verifiable by byte-equivalence rather than shape-equivalence.

design_facts.decision: MCP tool input schemas advertise the schema field as a free-form object; the JSON-Schema-lite subset is enforced at parse time by the parser, not at the MCP boundary.
design_facts.rationale: Avoids forcing every MCP client to import a schema-of-schema for marginal validation benefit.

contract.supported_constructs: Top-level object with typed scalar fields (string, number, boolean); optional enum constraint on string fields; optional required: [field, ...] array.
contract.rejection_modes: Returns a descriptive error for nested objects, arrays, anyOf/oneOf/allOf, $ref, format, additionalProperties: true, or any keyword outside the supported subset.

assertions: Parser accepts a schema object with typed scalar fields and returns a parsed schema value without error.
Parser accepts enum constraints on string fields per the feedback example shape (placement: a|b|c|d, confidence: low|med|high) and exposes the allowed values for downstream validation.
Parser rejects schema constructs outside the supported subset with a descriptive error.
Parser rejects nested objects, arrays, $ref, anyOf/oneOf/allOf, format, and additionalProperties:true with errors that name the offending construct.

contract.output_fence_style: Fenced JSON code block (```json ... ```). Validator extracts the last fenced JSON block in the response — panelists may write narrative before the final payload.
contract.suffix_contents: Enumerates each schema field with type; for enum fields, lists allowed values verbatim; explicit instruction to emit one final fenced JSON block matching the schema.
contract.determinism: Same parsed schema input produces byte-equivalent suffix output.

assertions: Given a parsed schema with enum-constrained fields, the builder produces a prompt suffix that names every field and its allowed values.
Given a parsed schema with free-text fields, the builder produces a prompt suffix that names every field and indicates free-text expected.
Suffix instructs panelists to wrap their structured response in a single fenced JSON code block; downstream validation treats the last such block as the canonical payload.
Builder is deterministic — repeated invocation with the same parsed schema returns byte-identical suffix output.

features.title: Per-panelist response validator with structured error surfacing

contract.result_extension: Two new fields on Result: Structured *json.RawMessage (json:"structured,omitempty") and StructuredError *ValidationError (json:"structured_error,omitempty"). Both nil when no schema was supplied — no wire-format change for the omitted-schema path.
contract.validation_input: Operates on the last fenced JSON code block in response (per F02). If no fenced block is found, emits a missing_fence error.
contract.error_shape: ValidationError{ Kind: string, Field: string, Message: string, Excerpt: string }. Kind in {"missing_fence", "json_parse", "schema_violation"}. Field is a dotted path (empty for top-level errors). Excerpt is at most 200 characters of the offending text.
contract.no_retry: Validation failure leaves Result.Response unchanged, Result.Structured nil, and Result.StructuredError populated; dispatch continues with no resend and no fallback prompt.

assertions: Given a panelist response that conforms to the schema, the validator returns the parsed structured value attached to the per-panelist result.
Given a panelist response that violates the schema, the validator surfaces the original raw text plus a structured error indicating which field(s) failed; no retry is invoked.
On validation failure, Result.Response retains the raw text unchanged, Result.Structured is nil, and Result.StructuredError carries Kind/Field/Message/Excerpt describing the failure.

When the dispatcher invokes the validator with a nil schema (omitted-schema path), the validator is not called; Result.Structured and Result.StructuredError remain nil and the wire format is byte-equivalent to current dispatch output.

features.title: Wire schema parameter into all five dispatch MCP tools

contract.mcp_param_type: object (free-form at the MCP input-schema level). The JSON-Schema-lite subset constraint is enforced at parse time by the parser; surfacing it at the MCP level would force every client to import the same schema-of-schema for marginal benefit.
contract.default_when_omitted: Parameter is optional. When absent: no prompt-suffix change, validator not invoked, Structured and StructuredError fields absent from per-panelist results.
contract.backwards_compat_bar: Byte-equivalent JSON output on the omitted-schema path. Verified by (a) the existing integration tests pass unmodified, (b) one new regression test asserts byte-equivalence between current dispatch and schema-omitted dispatch on identical inputs.
contract.error_propagation: A schema that fails parsing causes the dispatch tool to return an MCP-level error before any backend is invoked (fast fail; never partial dispatch with an invalid schema).

oracle.setup: MCP server built from internal/stdiomcp/server.go with all five dispatch tools registered.
oracle.tooling: Go integration tests against the MCP server entry point.
assertions: Each of the five dispatch MCP tools (roundtable-canvass, roundtable-deliberate, roundtable-blueprint, roundtable-critique, roundtable-crosscheck) accepts an optional schema input parameter declared in its MCP tool input schema.
When the schema parameter is omitted, each dispatch tool produces freeform markdown output identical to current behavior (backwards-compatible).
When the schema parameter is supplied, each dispatch tool composes the prompt suffix from F02, validates panelist responses via F03, and returns structured per-panelist values on the DispatchResult.
Backwards-compat regression: a new integration test asserts byte-equivalent JSON output between dispatch with schema omitted and current dispatch on identical inputs across all five tools.
When a malformed schema is supplied (fails the parser), the dispatch tool returns an MCP error before any backend is invoked.
